spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the headline capability the spec promises: detect platformversion via high-entropy ua-ch. Aligned with the chromestatus motivation.

interactive.rationale: Live interactive controls present and wired up — passes the no-static-card rule.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is well covered; edge cases / failure modes from the spec (see open questions) are not exercised on this concept page.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature link in the see-also section as the design system requires.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

openQuestions.title: Show the same probe pattern reused on Linux/Windows where platformVersion has different shape
openQuestions.detail: macOS support is the headline, but visitors on Windows or Linux should see something meaningful from the demo too — even a 'this is what macOS 12 users would see' synthetic input.

summary: UA-CH probe with high-entropy platformVersion read, in-page verdict. Main gap: Show the same probe pattern reused on Linux/Windows where platformVersion has different shape.
